signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Policy + Inflation + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7207 faces immediate volatility as CBA and markets price a 25bp hike to 4.35% at the May 5 meeting. April Monthly Inflation Gauge held steady at 4.3% y/y, confirming persistent price pressure. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 90% short—a massive institutional vs. retail divergence. Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.10% provides a -35bp carry advantage to AUD, which expands if RBA hikes. Retail capitulation risk is high if price clears 0.7250.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Geopolitical Headline
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5918 shows a sharp divergence between speculative positioning and central bank bias. COT LF net short -16,833 (specs fading the RBNZ) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias priced for the May 27 meeting. While RBNZ's Gai suggests the Hormuz supply shock does not warrant reflexive hikes, any hawkish confirmation at the meeting introduces significant short-squeeze risk for crowded spec shorts. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% maintains a +150bp USD carry advantage, but the positioning imbalance favors an NZD relief rally on hawkish hold.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.80 remains supported by the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry differential (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 reflects a crowded carry trade. While Trump's plan for the Strait of Hormuz introduces geopolitical tension, the VIX at 17.0 (normal volatility) suggests safe-haven JPY demand remains secondary to carry. Intervention risk remains the primary ceiling; watch for verbal warnings if price tests 160.00. Carry dominance persists unless VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1732 exhibits a significant correlation break with EuroStoxx (corr=+0.727 vs norm=-0.138, z=+1.89σ), indicating EUR is currently trading as a risk-on proxy rather than its historical inverse relationship with equities. COT LF net short -18,293 shows specs are positioned for downside, yet price has gained 1.22% over the last month. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp USD advantage) structurally limits upside, but the correlation shift suggests EUR may remain bid alongside equity strength in the near term.
